question.text: The J.R. Varma Committee was set up by SEBI in June 1998. What was its primary mandate?

answer.text: To recommend risk containment measures for the derivatives market
To develop the regulatory framework for derivatives trading
To draft amendments to the Securities Contracts (Regulation) Act
To recommend listing criteria for derivative products

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The J.R. Varma Committee was set up in June 1998 and submitted its report in October 1998. Its primary mandate was to recommend risk containment measures for the derivatives market, including margining systems and position limits. **The Trap:** "Developing the regulatory framework" was the mandate of the L.C. Gupta Committee, not the J.R. Varma Committee. Students frequently swap these two committee mandates. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series VIII tests the distinction between these committees clearly. L.C. Gupta = framework, J.R. Varma = risk containment. **Key Takeaway:** J.R. Varma Committee (June 1998, report October 1998) focused on risk containment measures — margins, position limits, and risk management for derivatives.
